danger_signals.visual_form: Past villains redesigned for 3D space within mind chambers
danger_signals.meaning: Threats to Jim's sanity; visual manifestation of internal fears

danger_signals.visual_form: Four mind chambers visually taken over by worst fears
danger_signals.meaning: Progressive psychological danger; loss of sanity if chambers not reclaimed

danger_signals.visual_form: Earthworm Kim (female evil alternate self) as final boss
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate internal threat; manifestation of Jim's deepest psychological trauma

safety_signals.visual_form: Golden Udders of Lucidity as collectible objects
safety_signals.meaning: Path to recovery; unlocks new mind chambers and restores sanity
Level progression safety; unlocks individual levels within recovered chambers

safety_signals.visual_form: Jim's primary blaster weapon maintained throughout
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and combat capability; consistent tool for defeating threats

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Primary playable areas; mind chambers gradually reclaimed
Allied entity working to stop subconscious madness

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Redesigned classic enemies occupying mind chambers
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Adapted to 3D form; visually distinct as occupants of fear territories
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Corruption of mind chambers; inhabit the four fear locations

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Evil female alternate self; dark mirror of protagonist

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Entire game world exists within Jim's mind; surreal subconscious environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game location is internal mental space; platform challenges reflect psychological struggle

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mind chambers corrupted by fears; visual chaos in environment design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Villains actively occupy chambers; madness threatens to trap Jim permanently

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mind chambers cleared of corruption; visual restoration of order
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Progression through level unlocks; path to next fear chamber opens

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Earthworm Kim revealed as personification of deepest trauma
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final boss encounter; ultimate threat to Jim's consciousness

environmental_storytelling.example: Four distinct mind chambers as organized zones of fear; each represents different aspect of Jim's trauma
Golden Udders unlock chambers (major story gates); Green Marbles unlock levels (finer narrative progression); visual keys to psychological recovery
Villains redesigned from 2D to 3D forms to inhabit Jim's subconscious; visual transformation reflects internal vs. external manifestation
Classic villains positioned as fears occupying specific mental territories; their presence indicates psychological danger zones

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Actively collecting Golden Udders and Green Marbles to unlock and clear mind chambers
Inhabit specific mind chamber zones; must be defeated to restore sanity
Actively working against madness; assists Jim's recovery efforts
Personifies deepest trauma; confronted only after other chambers cleared

notes: Earthworm Jim 3D communicates world-state primarily through psychological/internal metaphor. The entire game world is Jim's subconscious, making visual design inherently surreal. Danger signals center on mind corruption and villainous occupation of fear territories. Safety signals manifest as collectibles (Golden Udders, Green Marbles) that unlock recovery pathways. No traditional faction warfare or political conflict; instead, internal psychological conflict between sanity and madness. Visual storytelling relies on: (1) spatial organization of fears as chambers, (2) redesigned villain aesthetics for 3D/internal representation, (3) collectible-based narrative gates. The game lost promotional content during development (Evil the Cat replaced by Professor Monkey-for-a-Head as boss; ant-sized house level cut; Pocket Rocket racing removed), which may have included faction or political elements that were never realized in final build.
